skill.name: snowtower-maintainer
skill.description: Maintains SnowTower documentation, README content, and Claude configuration by guiding repository audits and documentation updates.
skill.summary: Guide SnowTower README, documentation, and Claude configuration maintenance.
skill.author: Database-Tycoon

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and weak-crypto patterns, but review found no malicious code or prompt injection. The weak-crypto alerts are false positives from Markdown text, while the shell commands are legitimate documentation-audit instructions that still require user oversight.

medium_findings.title: Local Shell Commands in Maintenance Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local commands such as uv, grep, find, ls, pytest, and pre-commit. These commands support documentation maintenance, but they execute project tooling and inspect local files, so users should review commands before allowing execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in Markdown maintenance procedures. They appear legitimate for the skill purpose, but execution of local tooling is a real capability with operational risk.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Inspection for Documentation Audits
low_findings.description: The skill asks agents to list directories and read documentation-related files to compare README, docs, workflows, and Claude configuration. This is expected for a maintainer skill, but it can expose local project structure to the agent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly involve local file and directory inspection. The purpose is documentation validation, and no data exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on Markdown lines that contain descriptions, path references, or documentation tables. No cryptographic implementation, hashing API, or security-sensitive algorithm use was found in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these are Markdown prose or path references, not cryptographic code. No evidence of weak cryptographic algorithm use was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple fenced bash examples tell the agent to execute local commands for health checks, README statistics, workflow checks, and agent audits. The pattern is not malicious, but it requires command review in community skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are explicit and repeated. They are consistent with repository maintenance, with no network calls or credential collection found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Statistics Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The README statistics task uses command substitution to count users, databases, and warehouses. This is a standard local reporting pattern, but command substitution can hide nested behavior if edited later.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command substitution syntax is present in the cited lines. Current commands only count local files, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

content.user_title: Maintain SnowTower Docs and Claude Config
content.value_statement: SnowTower documentation can drift from repository structure, commands, and Claude configuration. This skill guides focused audits that keep README, docs, agents, and patterns aligned.
actual_capabilities: Provides README audit steps for commands, badges, links, and project statistics.
Guides reviews of .claude skills, agents, patterns, and local settings references.
Maps documentation files to project sources that should remain synchronized.
Defines health-check commands for repository structure, tests, and pre-commit status.
Supports cleanup of obsolete documentation, removed features, and stale workflow names.
limitations: It is tailored to the SnowTower repository structure and file names.
It does not independently verify facts without repository access and command execution.
It does not include automated tests or scripts beyond Markdown command examples.
It should not run shell commands without user review in sensitive repositories.

use_cases.title: Refresh README Accuracy
use_cases.description: Check command references, workflow badges, links, and statistics before publishing documentation updates.

use_cases.title: Audit Claude Configuration
use_cases.description: Review .claude agents, skills, and reusable patterns for duplication, stale paths, and outdated descriptions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Documentation
use_cases.description: Run a release-focused documentation pass so guides, README content, and workflow references match current project behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check README Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the SnowTower README for outdated commands, broken internal links, stale workflow badges, and old statistics. Report proposed changes before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Sync Documentation with Project State
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the SnowTower docs against pyproject scripts, SnowDDL configuration paths, workflow files, and current guides. Update only documentation that is clearly stale.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Claude Folder Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Review .claude skills, agents, and patterns for duplicate responsibilities, incorrect paths, outdated descriptions, and missing references. Summarize findings by priority.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release Documentation Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release documentation audit for SnowTower. Verify README, guide pages, workflow references, agent descriptions, and project statistics. Produce a concise change plan.

output_examples.input: Audit the README before the next release.
output: Finds stale workflow badge references and outdated command descriptions.
Reports current project statistics that need confirmation.
Proposes focused README edits for review before applying them.

output_examples.input: Check whether the Claude agents need cleanup.
output: Identifies descriptions that reference old paths or removed capabilities.
Recommends consolidation steps and reference updates.

output_examples.input: Prepare documentation for a feature release.
output: Lists affected guide pages, README sections, and Claude configuration files.
Separates required updates from optional cleanup.
Highlights commands that need user approval before verification.

best_practices: Review shell commands before running them, especially in repositories with secrets or production configuration.
Make documentation edits in small groups that map to one verified repository change.
Keep README, guide pages, and Claude agent descriptions synchronized during each release.
anti_patterns: Do not update project statistics without verifying the current repository state.
Do not remove agents or patterns before checking references from README and documentation files.
Do not run broad maintenance commands in untrusted forks without user approval.

faq.question: What repository is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for SnowTower and assumes its README, docs, SnowDDL files, and .claude folder structure.

faq.question: Can it edit documentation automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, when used with an agent that has file access. Users should review proposed documentation changes before finalizing them.

faq.question: Does it execute code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown guidance, but it includes shell commands that an agent may run with user approval.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes local shell commands and filesystem inspection. They are legitimate, but they require command review.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests was found in the reviewed skill files.

faq.question: Can it maintain Claude Code configuration?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides audits of skills, agents, patterns, and references inside the .claude folder.
